volumeTitle: Casina. The Casket Comedy. Curculio. Epidicus. The Two Menaechmuses
label: Epidicus (Section 2.2.255 - Section 2.2.305b)

sections.label: Section 2.2.255
sections.original: Quid ego nunc faciam ? consilium a te expetesso , Apoecides .
sections.translation: What am I to do now ? I ask your advice , Apaecides .

sections.label: Section 2.2.256
sections.translation: Let us find some clever , useful expedient ; for he , indeed , will either be here just now , or is here already .

sections.label: Section 2.2.257b
sections.translation: If it were right for me to be wiser than you , I could give you some good advice , which you will praise , I fancy , both of you —

sections.label: Section 2.2.259b
sections.translation: Then where is it , Epidicus ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.260
sections.translation: Yes , and useful for this purpose ,

sections.label: Section 2.2.260b
sections.translation: Why do you hesitate to mention it ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.261
sections.original: Vos priores esse oportet , nos posterius dicere ,
sections.translation: It ’ s proper for yourselves , who are the wiser , to be the first to speak , and for me to speak afterwards .

sections.label: Section 2.2.262b
sections.translation: Aye , aye , of course — come , say on .

sections.label: Section 2.2.262c
sections.translation: But you ’ ll laugh at me .

sections.label: Section 2.2.263
sections.translation: On my word , we will not do so .

sections.label: Section 2.2.263b
sections.translation: Well then , if it pleases you , use my advice ; if it doesn ’ t please you , find better .

sections.label: Section 2.2.265
sections.original: mihi istic nec seritur nec metitur , nisi ea quae tu vis volo .
sections.translation: There ’ s neither sowing nor reaping [*] for me in this matter ; only that I do wish the same that you wish .

sections.label: Section 2.2.266
sections.original: Gratiam habeo ; fac participes nos tuae sapientiae .
sections.translation: I return you thanks . Make us partakers in your wisdom .

sections.label: Section 2.2.267
sections.original: Continuo arbitretur uxor tuo gnato atque ut fidicinam
sections.translation: Let a wife at once be chosen for your son ; and so take vengeance on this music-girl whom he wants to liberate , and who is corrupting him for you ; and so let it be managed , that even until her dying day she may remain a slave .

sections.label: Section 2.2.270
sections.translation: It ought to be so managed .

sections.label: Section 2.2.270b
sections.original: Facere cupio quidvis , dum id fiat modo .
sections.translation: I am ready to do anything , so long only as this may be brought about

sections.label: Section 2.2.270c
sections.translation: Well then , now there ’ s an opportunity of doing so , before he comes into the city , as to-morrow he will be here ; to-day he will not have come .

sections.label: Section 2.2.272b
sections.translation: How do you know ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.272c
sections.translation: I do know , because another person told me , who came from there , that he would be here in the morning .

sections.label: Section 2.2.274
sections.translation: Then say you what we are to do .

sections.label: Section 2.2.274b
sections.translation: I ’ m of opinion that you ought to do thus :

sections.label: Section 2.2.275
sections.original: quasi tu cupias liberare fidicinam animi gratia
sections.translation: you must pretend as though you were desirous to give her liberty to the music-girl for your own whim , and as though you were violently in love with her .

sections.label: Section 2.2.276b
sections.translation: To what advantage does that tend ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.276c
sections.translation: Do you ask that ? Why , that you may purchase her beforehand with money , before your son comes , and may say that you bought her to set her at liberty —

sections.label: Section 2.2.278b
sections.translation: I understand .

sections.label: Section 2.2.279
sections.original: Vbi erit empta , ut aliquo ex urbe amoveas ; nisi quid est tua
sections.translation: When she ’ s bought , you must remove her somewhere out of the city ; unless your own

sections.label: Section 2.2.280
sections.translation: feelings are any way opposed .

sections.label: Section 2.2.280b
sections.translation: O no , skilfully suggested .

sections.label: Section 2.2.280c
sections.translation: But what say you , Apaecides ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.281
sections.original: Quid ego iam nisi te commentum nimis astute intellego ?
sections.translation: Why , what should I ? Except that I think you ’ ve contrived it very cleverly .

sections.label: Section 2.2.282
sections.original: Iam simul igitur amota ei erit omnis consultatio
sections.translation: Then , in consequence , all thoughts of marriage with her will be removed from him , so that he will make no difficulties as to what you wish .

sections.label: Section 2.2.283b
sections.translation: Long life to you , wise as you are , it really does please us .

sections.label: Section 2.2.284b
sections.original: Tum tu igitur calide quidquid age .
sections.translation: Do you then skilfully do whatever you are going to do .

sections.label: Section 2.2.285
sections.translation: I ’ faith , you speak to the purpose .

sections.label: Section 2.2.285b
sections.original: Et repperi , haec te qui abscedat suspicio .
sections.translation: I have found , too , how this suspicion may be removed from yourself .

sections.label: Section 2.2.286
sections.translation: Let me know it .

sections.label: Section 2.2.286b
sections.translation: You shall know it ; just listen .

sections.label: Section 2.2.286c
sections.translation: He ’ s come with a breast full of counsel .

sections.label: Section 2.2.287
sections.original: Opus est homine qui illo argentum deferat pro fidicina ;
sections.translation: There ’ s need of a person to carry the money there for the music-girl ; but there ’ s no equal necessity for yourself to do it .

sections.label: Section 2.2.288b
sections.translation: Why so ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.288c
sections.translation: Lest he should think you are doing it for the sake of your son —

sections.label: Section 2.2.289b
sections.translation: Cleverly thought of !

sections.label: Section 2.2.289c
sections.translation: By which means you ’ ll keep him away from her ;

sections.label: Section 2.2.290
sections.original: ne qua ob eam suspicionem difficultas evenat .
sections.translation: lest any difficulty might arise by reason of that suspicion .

sections.label: Section 2.2.291
sections.original: Quém hominem inveniemus ad eam rem utilem ?
sections.translation: What person shall we find suited to this purpose ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.291b
sections.translation: He will be the best ; he will be able to take all due precautions , as he understands the laws and ordinances .

sections.label: Section 2.2.293
sections.translation: Epidicus , receive my thanks . But I ’ ll attend to this with all care .

sections.label: Section 2.2.293b
sections.translation: I ’ ll find him and bring him here to you , to whom the music-girl belongs ;

sections.label: Section 2.2.295
sections.original: atque argentum ego cum hoc feram .
sections.translation: and I ’ ll take the money along with him .

sections.label: Section 2.2.295b
sections.translation: For how much , at the lowest , can she be bought ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.295c
sections.translation: What , she ? Perhaps she might possibly be bought at the lowest for forty minae ; but if you give me more , I shall return it . There ’ s no trickery in this matter . This money , too , of yours won ’ t be locked up ten days .

sections.label: Section 2.2.299
sections.translation: How so ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.299b
sections.original: Quia enim mulierem alius illam adulescens deperit ,
sections.translation: Why , because another young man is dying with love for this woman ,

sections.label: Section 2.2.300
sections.original: auro opulentus , magnus miles Rhodius , raptor hostium ,
sections.translation: one abounding in money , a great warrior , a Rhodian , a spoiler of his foes [*] , a boaster ; he ’ ll buy her of you , and give the money with pleasure . You only do it ; there ’ s a large profit for you here .

sections.label: Section 2.2.302b
sections.translation: I really pray the Gods it may be so .

sections.label: Section 2.2.302c
sections.translation: You ’ ll obtain your prayer .

sections.label: Section 2.2.303
sections.original: Quin tu is intro atque huic argentum promis ? ego visam ad forum .
sections.translation: Why then , don ’ t you go in-doors and bring the money out here ? I ’ ll go visit the Forum . Epidicus , do you come thither .

sections.label: Section 2.2.304b
sections.original: Ne abitas prius quam ego ad te venero .
sections.translation: Don ’ t you go away from there before I come to you .

sections.label: Section 2.2.305
sections.translation: I ’ ll wait till then .

sections.label: Section 2.2.305b
sections.translation: Do you follow me in-doors .
